title: Vertical Definition
description: Definition of an industry vertical in the policy registry. Verticals are the shared vocabulary used across brand.json (brand.industry), campaign plans (plan.vertical), and policy entries (policy-entry.verticals). Every industry has a vertical definition; regulated industries have additional regulatory_frameworks and commonly_restricted_attributes. Non-regulated industries have empty arrays for those fields.

vertical_id.description: Unique identifier for this vertical. Used in plan.vertical and policy-entry.verticals for matching.

description.description: What this vertical covers and how to determine if a campaign belongs to it.

regulatory_frameworks.description: Key regulations and standards that apply to this vertical. Governance agents use this as context when evaluating campaigns in this vertical.

name.description: Name of the regulation or standard (e.g., 'US Fair Housing Act').

jurisdictions.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes where this framework applies.

summary.description: Brief summary of what the framework requires or prohibits for advertising in this vertical.

policy_ids.description: Registry policy IDs that implement this framework.

commonly_restricted_attributes.description: Restricted attribute categories that regulations commonly prohibit for this vertical. Informational — the plan's restricted_attributes field is authoritative, not this list.

guidance.description: Implementation notes for governance agents. Includes edge cases, disambiguation from similar verticals, and common pitfalls.

related_verticals.description: Verticals that frequently overlap with this one (e.g., 'credit' is related to 'financial_services' and 'housing'). When overlap exists, the plan should use the most specific vertical with the strictest regulatory requirements.
